channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA's DART spacecraft changed a binary asteroid's orbit around the sun, in a first for a human-made object - Engadget
item.description:
1. NASA's DART spacecraft changed a binary asteroid's orbit around the sun, in a first for a human-made object  Engadget
  2. NASA successfully kicks asteroid off course in Earth defence test  France 24
  3. NASA’s DART Mission Changed Orbit of Asteroid Didymos Around Sun  NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) (.gov)
  4. NASA DART mission reveals asteroids throw “cosmic snowballs” at each other  ScienceDaily
  5. You Can Probably Breathe Easier About Catastrophic Asteroid Collisions Now  WSJ

item.title: Stormy space weather may be garbling messages from aliens, new research suggests - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Stormy space weather may be garbling messages from aliens, new research suggests  The Guardian
  2. Missing technosignatures? Turbulent plasma may blur ultra-narrow signals before they leave their home star systems  Phys.org
  3. Where are all the aliens? Maybe space weather is scrambling their transmissions  Space
  4. Aliens may have been trying to contact Earth for years, scientists claim — here's why they’re not getting through  New York Post
  5. Why interstellar travel is impossible and aliens haven't visited Earth  AOL.com

item.title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing - NASASpaceFlight.com -
item.description:
1. NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing  NASASpaceFlight.com -
  2. NASA announces major overhaul of Artemis moon program amid safety concerns, delays: "We've got to get back to basics"  CBS News
  3. NASA Strengthens Artemis: Adds Mission, Refines Overall Architecture  NASA (.gov)
  4. 'Pushing this competition': SpaceX's Starship might not fly on NASA's newly revamped Artemis 3 mission  Space
  5. NASA has shuffled its Artemis rockets. But what of the lunar landers?  Ars Technica

item.title: Antarctica has a strange gravity hole and scientists finally know why - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. Antarctica has a strange gravity hole and scientists finally know why  ScienceDaily
  2. A Zone Under Antarctica Has the Weakest Gravity on Earth—and It’s Evolving, Scientists Say  Popular Mechanics
  3. Antarctica has a 'gravity hole' where sea levels are 420ft lower - and scientists finally know why  MSN
  4. Earth’s gravity is lumpy . “The gravity in East Antarctica is measurably weaker than anywhere else on the planet.”  Kottke.org
  5. Antarctica Sits Above Earth’s Strongest Gravity Hole  astrobiology.com

item.title: Enormous 3D map of the universe shows brilliant 'sea of light' near the cosmic dawn - Live Science
item.description:
1. Enormous 3D map of the universe shows brilliant 'sea of light' near the cosmic dawn  Live Science
  2. Astronomers unveil largest 3D universe map of its kind, illuminating 'hidden' cosmic structures  Space
  3. S&T physicist’s research helps reveal previously unseen galaxies  Missouri S&T
  4. Example spectrum of a Lyman alpha-emitting object (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!
  5. 3D Map of Early Universe Structures  Adafruit

item.title: Scientists May Have Just Proven Earth’s Real Age Thanks to Zircon Crystals, and It’s Far Older Than We Imagined! - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
1. Scientists May Have Just Proven Earth’s Real Age Thanks to Zircon Crystals, and It’s Far Older Than We Imagined!  Indian Defence Review
  2. Ancient Rocks Reveal Evidence Of The First Continents And Crust Recycling Processes On Earth  astrobiology.com
  3. Ancient Zircon Crystals Provide a Window into Early Earth History  Caltech
  4. A study based on the oldest rocks on the planet and computer modeling of the cratonic lithospheric mantle indicates that Earth's first continents were marked by extreme instability, raising new questions about how the planet evolved to become habitable  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. Earth’s oldest minerals suggest plate tectonics began 3.3 billion years ago  동아사이언스

item.description:

item.title: The Largest Black Hole Mergers Ever Detected: How 128 New Signals Are Expanding Our Universe - The Daily Galaxy
item.description:
1. The Largest Black Hole Mergers Ever Detected: How 128 New Signals Are Expanding Our Universe  The Daily Galaxy
  2. The universe is humming with ripples in spacetime: Scientists just doubled our catalog of black hole and neutron star collisions  Space
  3. New catalog more than doubles the number of gravitational-wave detections made by LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA observatories  MIT News
  4. Newly discovered ripples in spacetime put Einstein’s general relativity to the test  Scientific American
  5. Gravitational waves reveal hidden structure of galactic centers  Phys.org
